summary:
I've successfully identified and fixed the memory leak in the application. 

## Problem Analysis
The issue was in `app/processor.js` in the `DataProcessor` class. The `process()` method was registering new event listeners on every call without removing them:
- Each listener captured the input `data` parameter in its closure
- This caused memory to grow unboundedly as more requests were processed
- The closures prevented garbage collection of processed data

## Solution Applied
I moved the event listener registrations from the `process()` method to the constructor, so listeners are registered only once per processor instance instead of on every invocation.

## Verification
Running `test.sh` confirms the fix works:
- Listener count remains stable at 2 (no accumulation)
- Memory usage stays bounded  
- No event listener leak detected

The needle (memory leak) has been found and removed successfully. The application now properly handles multiple requests without growing memory usage over time.
